sections.paragraph:
with suff. also defect. דֹּדֹו, דֹּדְךָ m.

(1) love, only used in the plur. דֹּדִים, especially between the sexes, Son 1:2, 4:10 Eze 16:8 23:17 , מִשְׁכַּב דֹּדִים “bed of love;” Pro 7:18, לְכָה נִרְוֶה דֹדִים “come let us take our fill of love.” In some places tokens of love, caresses, kisses, are supposed to be the meaning, by Driessen in Dissertatt. Lugd. p. 1101, seq.

(2) as a concrete, object of love, one beloved, (compare وَدُّ, حِبُّ, حُبُّ love, and one loved, a friend, מֹודַעַת acquaintance, for an acquaintance, German, meine erfte Liebe, Bekanntfchaft, English, a relation of mine), Son 1:13, 14 , 16 2:3 , , , 10 , 16 , 17 5:5 .

(3) a friend, Isa 5:1. Specially a father’s brother, uncle by the father’s side; Syr. ܕܳܕܳܐ κατʼ ἐξοχὴν called the friend of the family, like the Ch. הֲבִיבָא a friend, hence a paternal uncle; comp. חֲבִיבְתָּא a mother-in-law; Germ. Freund, used of a relation; Latin amita qs. amata. Lev 10:4 20:20 1Sa 10:14 15:16 Est 2:15 Jer 32:7, Jer 32:8, Jer 32:9. In verse 12 Jer 32:12, indeed, it seems to be put for בֶּן דֹּור.
